proof_boundary: This artifact rules out the specific convergent unit-atomic elementary multiplier product for the normalized lambda-zero zeta coefficients. It does not rule out every multiplier sequence or other all-order Jensen/PF bridges, and does not prove PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda <= 0.

rows.claim: A unit atom contributes the positive Hausdorff difference kernel.
The target product would express every first difference moment as a unit sum.
rows.proof_boundary: This is precisely the unit-atomic hypothesis being tested.

rows.claim: No unit atom can lie at or below the certified cutoff.
rows.formula: g_6(4.863538496)>a_6 implies alpha_j>4.863538496 for every j
rows.proof_boundary: Uses unit multiplicity; arbitrary subunit weights are not excluded.

rows.claim: The consecutive one-atom ratio is strictly decreasing in alpha.
If all target atoms exceed the cutoff, the total ratio is capped by the cutoff atom ratio.
rows.proof_boundary: Positive weighted-average consequence of the target hypothesis.

rows.claim: The actual coefficient ratio is strictly above the required cap.
rows.proof_boundary: Arb contradiction at lambda=0 using A_0..A_57 sources.

rows.claim: The normalized zeta coefficient sequence has no admissible unit-atomic elementary multiplier product.
rows.formula: not exists {alpha_j}: y_k=sum_j -log(1-1/(k+alpha_j)^2), alpha_j>0, sum_j alpha_j^(-2)<infinity
rows.proof_boundary: Rejects this sufficient subclass, not all multiplier sequences.

rows.claim: The multiplier counting-measure route is retired; other all-order Jensen/PF routes remain open.
rows.formula: unit-atomic route closed as false; general bridge targets unchanged
rows.proof_boundary: Not PF-infinity, the all-order Jensen bridge, RH, or Lambda <= 0.

status: interval-certified unit-atomic obstruction; not a proof of PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda <= 0
